name: Grounding DINO
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); IDEA Research; GPU recommended

performance.notes: ~200ms per image on GPU; open-vocabulary detection slower than YOLO

agent_readiness.notes: Open-vocabulary object detection model — detect any object described in natural language. No fixed class list. Combined with SAM for Grounded SAM (detect + segment anything with text). Used in visual agents for object localization, automated annotation, zero-shot detection. DINO stands for Detection Transformer with Improved deNoising training. IDEA Research (Beijing).

gotchas: Slower than YOLO for closed-set detection tasks
Detection quality varies by query specificity — vague text queries unreliable
Installation can be complex (CUDA compilation required)
best_when: Zero-shot detection of arbitrary objects without training
Visual agents that need to locate specific objects from text description
Combined with SAM for open-vocabulary segmentation (Grounded SAM)
avoid_when: Simple use cases where YOLO or CLIP zero-shot suffices
summary: Grounding DINO enables zero-shot open-vocabulary object detection using natural language queries. Detect any object described in text without fixed class lists. Combined with SAM for Grounded SAM (detect + segment with text). Used in visual agents for object localization and automated annotation. Slower than YOLO for fixed classes — best for arbitrary object detection without training.
